humanSummary.text: Initial audits found P1 symlink escape and root-adoption cleanup risks. The branch repaired both and re-audit found no remaining P0/P1 blockers. Remaining P2/P3 hardening issues are recorded as DAG follow-ups.

findings.title: Path-based runtime artifact defense retains TOCTOU race
findings.description: The repaired implementation rejects existing symlink parents and destinations, but still validates paths before later path-based rename/read_dir operations. A malicious concurrent actor could swap a validated directory for a symlink between validation and use.

evidence.detail: Focused re-audit identified residual TOCTOU windows in write and cleanup paths and recommended fd-relative/no-follow hardening as follow-up work.

findings.impact: The foundation is safe for non-malicious local use but not yet hardened against concurrent filesystem mutation inside the artifact root.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Artifact writes use fd-relative/no-follow operations or an equivalent directory capability API.
Cleanup traverses only verified managed-root directory handles or an equivalent no-follow mechanism.
Regression tests cover symlink root, symlink marker, symlink parent, symlink destination, and concurrent-swap approximations where practical.

acceptanceCriteria: Runtime artifact writes and cleanup are hardened against TOCTOU symlink swaps using fd-relative/no-follow operations or equivalent capabilities.
auditFocus: Concurrent symlink swap between validation and use
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node UTSUSHI-093 records this non-blocking hardening finding.

findings.title: Runtime artifact DB constraint is weaker than repository URI policy
findings.description: The DB check constraint enforces the managed prefix and rejects schemes, absolute paths, and backslashes, but does not reject dot segments, dot-dot segments, empty segments, or all malformed refs rejected by repository and Rust validation.

evidence.detail: Architecture audit compared migration constraints against repository and Rust validation and found direct SQL writes can persist malformed managed artifact refs.

findings.impact: Direct database writes can bypass runtime artifact URI policy and persist refs that application code would reject.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: DB constraints reject the same invalid runtime artifact ref examples as repository validation.
Direct-SQL tests cover dot, dot-dot, empty segment, scheme, absolute path, backslash, and missing-prefix cases.

acceptanceCriteria: Database constraints and repository validation reject the same malformed runtime artifact refs.
auditFocus: Direct SQL bypass of runtime artifact ref policy
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node UTSUSHI-094 records this non-blocking parity finding.

findings.title: Runtime artifact docs overstate shared v0.2 evidence naming
findings.description: The new docs state all v0.2 runtime artifact refs use the managed runtime prefix, while existing shared schema fixtures still use older artifacts/utsushi/hello refs. The boundary between bridge evidence refs and Itotori managed storage refs needs clarification or fixture migration.

evidence.detail: Architecture audit found docs and shared runtime evidence fixtures using different artifact ref conventions.

findings.impact: Future agents may update schema fixtures, DB ingestion, or docs inconsistently because the ownership boundary for managed storage refs is unclear.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Docs and fixtures agree on whether artifacts/utsushi/runtime is required by v0.2 evidence or only by Itotori managed storage.
Tests cover any conversion boundary between bridge evidence refs and managed storage refs.

acceptanceCriteria: Runtime artifact docs and bridge-schema fixtures agree on the managed storage prefix boundary.
auditFocus: Docs and schema fixture artifact ref drift
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Follow-up node UTSUSHI-095 records this non-blocking documentation/contract finding.

findings.title: Symlink managed-root and marker rejection need focused tests
findings.description: The implementation checks symlink managed-root paths and marker files, but tests currently focus on artifact parent and destination symlinks. Additional coverage would keep managed-root and marker checks from regressing.

evidence.detail: Focused re-audit noted the code checks symlink root and marker cases but the new symlink tests cover parent and destination cases only.

findings.impact: A future refactor could weaken root or marker symlink rejection without failing the current targeted tests.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Tests reject a symlink used as the managed runtime artifact root.
Tests reject a symlink used as the managed-root marker file.

acceptanceCriteria: Runtime artifact hardening tests cover symlink managed-root paths and symlink marker files.
auditFocus: Untested symlink root and marker rejection paths
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Folded into UTSUSHI-093 because it belongs with runtime artifact no-follow hardening.
